channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Menendez brothers’ house is all over TikTok following the hit Netflix drama
item.description: With the recent release of the Netflix drama Monsters: The Lyle and Erik Menendez Story and the follow-up documentary The Menendez Brothers, true-crime enthusiasts are now turning their attention to the Beverly Hills mansion where it all went down.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Global cybersecurity talent gap widens
item.description: Despite some recent positive news for tech hiring, a growing cybersecurity talent shortage threatens to hamstring businesses. Data from CyberSeek shows that in the U.S., some 265,000 more cybersecurity workers are needed to solve current staffing needs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta lays off staff at WhatsApp and Instagram to align with 'strategic goals'
item.description: Meta says it has laid off some employees, including staff at WhatsApp And Instagram, to realign its resources with its “strategic goals.”.
item.dc:creator: Zen Soo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Digital addiction detox: Streamline tech to maximize impact, minimize risks
item.description: Forty-one percent of organizations adopted and used digital platforms for all or most functions in 2024, compared with just 26% in 2023, according to IDC’s May 2024 Future Enterprise Resiliency and Spending Survey, Wave 5.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Data center provider fakes Tier 4 data center certificate to bag $11M SEC deal
item.description: Deepak Jain, CEO of a Maryland-based IT services firm, has been indicted for fraud and making false statements after allegedly falsifying a Tier 4 data center certification to secure a $10.7 million contract with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI 네이티브 소프트웨어 엔지니어링 시대, 생각보다 빨리 올 수도
item.description: 가트너의 소프트웨어 엔지니어링 부문 수석 책임 애널리스트인 필립 월시는 많은 개발 작업을 완전히 자동화하거나 부분적으로 도울 수 있는 AI 코딩 에이전트가 등장하고 있다며, 2025년에는 더 많은 파일럿 프로그램과 개념 증명이 출시될 것으로 예상된다고 말했다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Secure Azure Kubernetes with Advanced Container Networking Services
item.description: Microsoft’s Azure Kubernetes Service is designed to take care of much of the day-to-day management of Kubernetes, simplifying a lot of the complexity that goes with building and running large-scale, cloud-native applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: VM웨어 구독 서비스 두고 분쟁 벌인 AT&T-브로드컴··· 합의 근접
item.description: VM웨어(VMware) 지원 서비스를 둘러싼 AT&T와 브로드컴 간의 법적 분쟁이 해결될 조짐을 보이고 있다. 지난 11일 뉴욕 대법원에 제출된 공동 서한에 따르면, 양사는 합의에 진전을 이루고 있어 예정된 청문회 연기를 요청했다. 이번 분쟁은 브로드컴의 VM웨어 인수에 따른 지원 서비스 연장 문제를 중심으로 전개되었으며, 기업 IT 지원 계약 전반에 미칠 잠재적 영향으로 주목받았다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Anthropic Updates Responsible Scaling Policy to Address Catastrophic Risks in AI
item.description: Anthropic updates its Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP) to address catastrophic risks from frontier AI systems, emphasizing safety and security measures as the company pushes to be perceived as an AI safety first provider.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Feds seeks more guidance on post-quantum cryptography transition
item.description: Federal cyber experts say they need more formal guidance and a range of other tools for a post-quantum cryptography effort that's expected to cost billions. The post Feds seeks more guidance on post-quantum cryptography transition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: What are VTubers? The controversial class of streamers, explained
item.description: VTubers, the internet creators who use digital avatars while live-streaming, blew up on YouTube in the late 2010s. Since then, the streamers have expanded across other platforms. Twitch’s most popular streamer is VTuber Ironmouse, with 236,505 active subscribers.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Is ghosting someone online ever OK?
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: DoNotPay will now call customer service hotlines for you
item.description: If you dread the thought of calling to change an airline ticket or negotiate your internet bill, a new artificial intelligence tool may provide a solution.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Trump promotes family’s cryptocurrency tokens
item.description: Former President Trump touted his family’s new cryptocurrency tokens, encouraging users to “embrace this incredible technology” in a video message Tuesday. The WLFI tokens, released Tuesday morning, are part of the Trump family’s new crypto endeavor, World Liberty Financial.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: 5 ways to stop Windows Update from rebooting your PC
item.description: Windows Update can be a pain. Rebooting for updates is one thing — but a forced reboot for updates that shut down your running applications when you’re trying to get work done? Now, that’s obnoxious. Windows Update hit rock bottom in the early years of Windows 10.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spotify is adding new markets to its music video test
item.description: Spotify is expanding its beta test of adding music videos to the platform by several dozen regions, though it still is unavailable in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk loaded $75 million into his pro-Trump super PAC in 3 months
item.description: Elon Musk has personally contributed at least $75 million to his pro-Trump America PAC since July as he pushes for the former president to return to the White House, according to campaign finance records filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he likes Mark Zuckerberg 'much better now'
item.description: Former President Trump said he likes Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg “much better now,” as the head of Facebook and Instagram increasingly looks to stay out of politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google endorses California GOP Rep. Issa’s bill to reveal funders backing lawsuits
item.description: The bill, introduced last week would require plaintiffs to reveal any investors who could make money based on the outcome of a case. It would also require parties to disclose the financing agreement between investors and those involved.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to leverage APIs for IT-enabled information capability
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses face constant pressure to adapt or risk being left behind. The strategic use of IT-enabled information management capability (IMC) is no longer just an advantage — it’s a necessity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Connect in a crisis with iOS 18's new video call feature for emergency services
item.description: Apple’s new Emergency SOS Live Video feature allows users to share live video footage of the emergency directly with first responders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoT driving IT modernization to meet enterprise, local needs
item.description: Cordell Schachter, the Transportation Department CIO, said the agency’s DevSecOps capabilities are maturing as part of its digital transformation effort. The post DoT driving IT modernization to meet enterprise, local needs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
